title: Simple VR
description: Lightweight VR Vivecraft modpack for optimized vanilla Minecraft experience
body:
# Optimized VR Modpack for Minecraft

> Smooth, stable, and ready-to-play VR with **Vivecraft** on the most popular Minecraft versions.

## ✨ Key Features
- **VR-first tuning**: low latency, reduced stutter, motion-comfort presets.
- **Vivecraft core**: full head tracking, motion controllers, room-scale.
- **Performance mods only**: minimal, conflict-free, fast loading.
- **Works out of the box**: smart defaults; advanced options for power users.
- **Actively targeted versions**: focuses on the most played Minecraft releases.

## 🧰 Requirements
- PC VR setup (Valve Index / HTC Vive / Meta Quest via PCVR / WMR).
- Java 17+ (for newer Minecraft versions) or matching Java per version.
- GPU with VR-ready performance (NVIDIA/AMD with up-to-date drivers).

## 📦 What's Inside (Curated)
- **Vivecraft** (VR support & input)
- Lightweight **performance & QoL** helpers (only essentials, no bloat)
- Optional **reprojection/ASW-friendly** presets

> Exact mod list may vary by version to maintain stability and performance.

## 🧭 Installation (Modrinth)
1. Install the **Modrinth App** (or your preferred launcher with Modrinth support).
2. Open the project page, click **Install** for your desired Minecraft version.
3. Launch the instance and put on your VR headset.
4. In-game: open **Vivecraft Settings → VR**, verify controller bindings.

**Alternative:** Import the `.mrpack` file into Prism Launcher (Add Instance → Import).

## ⚙️ Recommended Settings
- **Render Distance**: 8–12 chunks (start low, scale up).
- **FOV / IPD**: leave defaults, fine-tune in VR menu.
- **Motion Comfort**: enable vignette & snap-turn if prone to motion sickness.
- **ASW/Reprojection**: allow your GPU software to manage for smoother frames.

## 🔄 Supported Versions
- Current focus on **the most popular Minecraft versions** for best mod support.
- Use the **matching modpack release** for your version (check file name & changelog).

## 🛠 Troubleshooting
- **Black screen / long load**: update GPU drivers and Java.
- **Controller issues**: re-run SteamVR bindings and check Vivecraft input settings.
- **Performance dips**: lower render distance, disable heavy shaders, close background programs.

## 🙌 For Whom
- Players who want **plug-and-play VR** in Minecraft.
- Creators and streamers seeking **stable, smooth VR** with minimal setup.
- Anyone new to **Vivecraft** who prefers a clean, curated pack.

## 📜 Notes
- Respect each mod’s license and the Modrinth distribution terms.
- Report issues with logs, headset model, GPU, and Minecraft version.

Although this modpack was designed for [fluffycraft.net](https://fluffycraft.net/), it is fully universal. You can use it on other servers or in single-player mode—it acts as a complete, standalone pack and is not locked to a specific project
